steps.title: Set up the unit conversion
steps.content:
Acceleration has dimensions distance/time². We need to convert both parts:
  • Distance: 1 mile = 1,609 m, so multiply by $\frac{1}{1609}$
  • Time: 1 min = 60 s, so 1 min² = 3,600 s². When converting per-second² to per-minute², multiply by 3,600
The combined conversion is:
$$3.2 \text{ m/s}^2 \times \frac{1}{1609} \times 3600$$

steps.title: Calculate and identify the choice
steps.content:
$$\frac{3.2 \times 3600}{1609} = \frac{11520}{1609}$$
Computing: $11520 \div 1609 \approx 7.16$

Rounded to the nearest tenth: 7.2. That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
